title: moraea minor
intro: Moraea minor, also known as the yellow pincushion or yellow corn lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, native to southern Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Moraea minor can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
